siteResults.findings: Non-healing mixed red-white patch with irregular margins and contact bleeding concern.
Diffuse staining and frictional change without focal ulceration.
Small keratotic area requiring rescreen and comparison with prior visit.
No focal suspicious lesion visible in selected frame.

hypotheses.label: Potentially malignant oral disorder
hypotheses.rationale: High-risk smokeless tobacco exposure plus red-white left buccal lesion and interval growth.

hypotheses.label: Traumatic keratosis or frictional lesion
hypotheses.rationale: Some white plaque features may be friction-related, but growth and erythema keep concern elevated.

hypotheses.label: Inflammatory ulcer
hypotheses.rationale: Inflammation remains possible but persistence and habit history make referral appropriate.

delta.summary: Left buccal lesion grew 4mm since previous visit.

carePlan.patientMessage: Please visit the free doctor camp this week. The ASHA will help with the referral.
carePlan.ashaMessage: Prioritize referral. Prepare anonymized package with left buccal ROI, interval-growth note, and risk history.
carePlan.doctorBrief: Anonymized screening visit: high-risk smokeless tobacco exposure, left buccal red-white lesion, 4mm interval growth, referral recommended.

thinking: The assessment weighs visible lesion morphology, TSNA-weighted chewing exposure, uncertainty at secondary sites, and documented interval growth.
citations: WHO oral cancer prevention and early detection guidance
